equivalence_argument: ExecutorRegistryConfig::from_env exists, but it reads different environment variables and omits TTL entirely.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: from_env does not read TEEDA_CONTRACT_ADDRESS, EXECUTOR_REGISTRY_MAX_SIZE, or EXECUTOR_REGISTRY_TTL with the required Address::ZERO/1000/3600 fallback behavior.
related_but_insufficient_code: ExecutorRegistryConfig has contract_address and max_executors only; absent or invalid address falls back to 0x9644e8562cE0Fe12b4deeC4163c064A8862Bf47F rather than Address::ZERO, and there is no TTL field.

rationale: The commit adds a dedicated from_env constructor and explicitly defines the environment variable names, default values, parse behavior, and invalid address fallback. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: ExecutorRegistryConfig::from_env must read TEEDA_CONTRACT_ADDRESS, EXECUTOR_REGISTRY_MAX_SIZE, and EXECUTOR_REGISTRY_TTL from the process environment; absent or unparsable size and TTL values must fall back to 1000 and 3600 respectively, and an absent or invalid contract address must produce Address::ZERO.
suggested_falsification_tests: Run isolated tests with env vars unset, set to valid values, and set to invalid strings; assert from_env returns Address::ZERO/1000/3600 for unset or invalid fallbacks and the parsed address, max_executors, and executor_ttl_seconds for valid values.
summary: Executor registry env defaults do not match the invariant contract.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Run from_env with TEEDA_CONTRACT_ADDRESS, EXECUTOR_REGISTRY_MAX_SIZE, and EXECUTOR_REGISTRY_TTL unset/invalid/valid and assert Address::ZERO/1000/3600 fallback semantics; current code uses different variables and no TTL.
title: Executor registry config honors env defaults
